taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: SQL, Tableau, Python, R, Data analysis
skill_match_scores: SQL:1.0, Tableau:0.5, Python:1.0, R:0.0, Data analysis:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, etc.), 1-2 years of experience or coursework using SQL to manipulate and analyze large datasets, Strong analytical thinking with ability to move from raw data to insight and recommendation, Effective communication skills to explain analytical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences
preferred_quals: Familiarity with Python or R for deeper analysis, Experience with BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, etc.)
core_responsibilities: Use SQL to analyze guest-level transactions and behavioral data to uncover trends in frequency, retention, and engagement, Support the definition and standardization of guest-level metrics and the development of segmentation frameworks, Partner with cross-functional teams to measure campaign impact and guest-level incrementality, Contribute to test-and-learn frameworks to evaluate initiatives and improve marketing effectiveness, Proactively identify opportunities to improve guest experience and business performance through data, Collaborate with Data Engineering team to ensure guest data is reliable, structured, and scalable, Create clear, compelling visualizations and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders, Support the enhancement of reporting tools and methods to improve data storytelling
job_summary: Burger King is part of Restaurant Brands International Inc., one of the world's largest quick service restaurant companies. As an Analyst, Guest Intelligence, you will analyze guest-level data to uncover insights that drive growth, retention, and marketing effectiveness, translating data into actionable recommendations for CRM and promotional strategies.
